data.title: 'Future looks very bright,' JPM upgrades Renk and doubles PT
data.description:
** J.P.Morgan upgrades German defence supplier Renk to "overweight" from "neutral" citing strong orders and improved operational performance over the last two quarters

** JPM says that Renk, which makes gearboxes for Leopard 2 tanks and transmissions for Bradley fighting vehicles, is well-positioned to benefit from growing defence spending in Europe, Asia and the Middle East

** "Given the extremely strong outlook for European defence spending (especially in Germany) and very strong recent orders for armoured vehicles containing RENK's products, the future looks very bright for RENK, in our view" - JPM

** It expects Renk to achieve an 18% sales compound annual growth rate from 2024 to 2030, with further upside from potential acquisitions

** The brokerage notes as well that the company is addressing operational issues in its U.S. business which have weighed on its earnings, and anticipates EBITA margins in the U.S. to return to over 20% in coming years

** JPM doubles its PT on the stock to 70 euros, which it says implies about 25% upside potential over the next 18 months

** Renk's shares are up 2.8% in early Frankfurt trade and are seen at the top of German small-cap index

data.title: Sunpower's Attributable Profit Jumps 29% in Q1
data.description:
Sunpower's attributable profit to equity holders surged 29% during the first quarter of the year to 72.2 million yuan from 56.0 million yuan a year earlier, according to a Thursday filing on the Singapore Exchange.

Earnings per share rose to 0.0908 yuan compared with 0.0704 yuan in the comparable period.

Revenue declined 4% year over year to 793.1 million yuan from 825.8 million yuan, due to price adjustment because of a decrease in fuel costs.

data.title: Europe before the bell: Stocks set to round off positive week in the green
data.description:
EUROPE BEFORE THE BELL: STOCKS SET TO ROUND OFF POSITIVE WEEK IN THE GREEN

European equity futures are higher on Friday as markets look set to round off another week in the green, due to easing trade tensions and expectations of large U.S. tax cuts.

Euro STOXX 50 futures (STXec1) are up 0.2%, as the index looks to add to its almost-2% gain this week. A close higher on Friday would mark its fifth week of gains.

Major index futures in Frankfurt , Paris (FCEc1) and London (FCIc1) are all up about 0.3%.

Overnight in Asia, shares were mixed, but have had a stellar week more broadly, with MSCI's main gauge of Asia-Pacific stocks ex-Japan (.MIAPJ0000PUS) up over 3%. Wall Street futures are little changed on Friday.

In corporate news, luxury group Richemont reported a slightly stronger-than-forecast 7% rise in quarterly sales.

Reinsurer Swiss Re reported a 16% rise in quarterly profit, even after huge claims relating to the deadly wildfires in Los Angeles this year.

(Samuel Indyk)

*****

FRIDAY'S OTHER LIVE MARKETS POSTS:

TRADE-DRIVEN RALLY ENDS THE WEEK WITH A WHIMPER CLICK HERE

data.title: Staffline Group Contract Could Be With Muller's Culina, Panmure Says — Market Talk
data.description: Staffline Group's new contract could be worth 300 million pounds and generate 100 million pounds of revenue a year, Panmure Liberum analysts write. They also believe the contract is with Culina, which is owned by Muller. The recruiting firm said it agreed a new contract with an unnamed leading food and drink logistics provider. It said the contract has an initial two-year period with an option to extend by an extra year, but didn't disclose any financial terms. Based on their valuation the analysts say the contract is the largest the company has won and increases its share in the blue-collar industrial market to 49% in FY24 from 22% in FY22. Shares closed at 27.2 pence Thursday and are up 16% over the year to date. (ian.walker@wsj.com.)

data.title: Richemont Investors to Welcome Results as Jewelry Momentum Continues — Market Talk
data.description: Richemont's results confirm strong revenue momentum at its core jewelry division in its fiscal fourth quarter and are likely to be well received by investors, RBC Capital Markets' Piral Dadhania says in a note to clients. "We expect these results to be positively received, particularly in the context of a challenging broader luxury environment. The outlook should also be supported by recent price increases at Cartier (mid-May) and Van Cleef (mid-April)," the analyst says. The Swiss jeweler and watchmaker reported a 11% rise in fourth-quarter revenue for its jewelry business, beating consensus expectations of a 9% rise, the analyst says. By region, Richemont's quarterly sales missed expectations in Asia-Pacific, but was stronger than anticipated elsewhere, RBC says. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0648 GMT - Richemont's results confirm strong revenue momentum at its core jewelry division in its fiscal fourth quarter and are likely to be well received by investors, RBC Capital Markets' Piral Dadhania says in a note to clients. "We expect these results to be positively received, particularly in the context of a challenging broader luxury environment. The outlook should also be supported by recent price increases at Cartier (mid-May) and Van Cleef (mid-April)," the analyst says. The Swiss jeweler and watchmaker reported a 11% rise in fourth-quarter revenue for its jewelry business, beating consensus expectations of a 9% rise, the analyst says. By region, Richemont's quarterly sales missed expectations in Asia-Pacific, but was stronger than anticipated elsewhere, RBC says. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0648 GMT - Staffline Group's new contract could be worth 300 million pounds and generate 100 million pounds of revenue a year, Panmure Liberum analysts write. They also believe the contract is with Culina, which is owned by Muller. The recruiting firm said it agreed a new contract with an unnamed leading food and drink logistics provider. It said the contract has an initial two-year period with an option to extend by an extra year, but didn't disclose any financial terms. Based on their valuation the analysts say the contract is the largest the company has won and increases its share in the blue-collar industrial market to 49% in FY24 from 22% in FY22. Shares closed at 27.2 pence Thursday and are up 16% over the year to date. (ian.walker@wsj.com.)

0640 GMT - Swiss Re's first-quarter results were unusually benign when looking beyond the California wildfires, Jefferies says in a research note. "Although natural catastrophe losses cost Swiss Re's Property & Casualty Re business $570 million in 1Q 2025, far more than the seasonal budget of $363 million, this loss burden was still 20% below consensus, and thus a key driver behind the group beating consensus by 36%," analyst Philip Kett writes. Its life and health reinsurance claims also contributed to the better result. While insurance revenue across business lines looks somewhat light, there is little not to like about the print, he adds. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0638 GMT - Swiss Re profits beat consensus comfortably but this was driven by a higher investment result and lighter-than-expected natural catastrophe losses, Keefe, Bruyette & Woods says after the reinsurer's first-quarter results. The combined ratio of its property and casualty reinsurance division was higher than consensus, which could be explained by the nominal catastrophe losses being lower than expectations despite being higher than what was budgeted. "Similarly to the other reinsurers, we think the market may be disappointed by the April renewals," analyst Darius Satkauskas writes in a research note. He also notes that though the headline price rose by 1.5%, the loss cost assumptions rose 3.7%, which leads to a negative 2.3% net price change. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0633 GMT - Airports of Thailand faces an earnings hit from likely reduced passenger flows, Nomura analysts say in a research report. Expectations of a tourism slowdown in Thailand prompt the brokerage to cut FY 2025 and FY 2026 passenger-count assumptions for the airport operator by 7.6% and 6.6%, respectively. Hence, Nomura's earnings forecasts for AOT are lowered by 46.8% for FY 2025 and 47% for FY 2026. However, the brokerage notes that the sharp fall in the airport operator's share price since news of a liquidity crunch at its concessionaire has fully priced in consensus earnings reductions. Nomura maintains a buy rating on the stock and lowers the target price to THB44.00 from THB78.00. Shares are 1.4% higher at THB35.50. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0628 GMT - Nongshim's earnings are expected to improve from 2Q on lower marketing costs and a price increase in March for instant-noodle products in South Korea, Korea Investment & Securities analyst Kang Eun-ji writes in a note. The South Korean food company's increased spending on promoting its new products, especially in the U.S., is set to gradually decrease from 2Q, Kang says. She expects 2Q operating profit to rise 11% from a year earlier, after falling 8.7% in 1Q. Korea Investment raises its target price for the stock by 6.7% to KRW480,000 and keeps a buy rating. Shares are 9.4% higher at KRW454,000. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

0614 GMT - Nordic markets are seen opening slightly higher, with IG calling the OMXS30 up 0.1% at around 2545. The S&P 500 rose 0.4% yesterday, marking the fourth consecutive day of gains, while the Nasdaq fell 0.2%, SEB analysts ay in a note. "Relative optimism has gained momentum after Trump and China's tariff easing from earlier in the week, although some caution among investors could be indicated after a rise in dividend-rich stocks that underperformed earlier in the month." In Asia, the stock markets opened lower. Talks between Russian and Ukrainian delegations may take place in Istanbul today, but expectations are low, after both countries' leaders chose not to participate, SEB adds. OMXS30 closed at 2542.32, OMXN40 at 2383.45 and OBX at 1464.28. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

0602 GMT - Assets under administration at U.K. investment platforms were roughly stable over the first quarter as the market yo-yoed against a volatile geopolitical backdrop, data compiled by Fundscape shows. Assets edged down 0.1% on quarter to 1.142 trillion pounds at the end of March. Gross flows were 46.9 billion pounds, while net flows amounted to 11.3 billion pounds--the best quarterly performance in three years, according to Fundscape's report. The top five platforms by net flows across all channels were Quilter, followed by AJ Bell, Hargreaves Lansdown, Aviva and Fidelity. "Harder to predict is what will happen in Q2--Trump's Liberation Day coincided with the last week of the tax year, so [individual savings accounts] activity is likely to have been hit hard," Fundscape CEO Bella Caridade-Ferreira says. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0545 GMT - Manila Electric appears to lack any further rerating catalysts, SB Equities' Katrine Eunice Dolatre says in a research report. Its share price has risen 18% year-to-date through May 14, thanks to recent announcements including franchise renewal, the analyst notes. Also, its share price looks a bit overvalued based on the current price-to-earnings and price-to-book-value ratios, the analyst says. Hence, the brokerage downgrades the stock's rating to hold from add. However, given SB Equities makes no adjustments to its current assumptions about the Philippine electric distribution utility, the brokerage maintains the target price at PHP562.80. Shares are 1.3% lower at PHP562.50. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0532 GMT - Alibaba's cloud business revenue growth is likely to continue to accelerate, according to Daiwa analyst John Choi in a research note. Daiwa forecasts the company's cloud revenue to see an over 20% on-year growth in fiscal 1Q, following an 18% growth in the previous quarter. Despite the rise in research and development and other expenses, its cloud EBITA margin is likely to maintain a stable high-single-digit percentage margin in the coming quarters, with AI application demand continuing to rise, Daiwa says. The analyst keeps a buy rating and slightly cuts target price to HK$170.00 from HK$175.00. Shares are last traded at HK$123.30.(tracy.qu@wsj.com)

0515 GMT - Singapore Post may be profitable in FY 2026, but the focus is in monetization of its assets, Maybank Research's Jarick Seet says in a research report. Management will likely quicken its efforts to further reduce the size and scale of its loss-making postal network and sell some properties to unlock value for shareholders, the analyst says. Also, the company will probably engage with the Singapore authorities more actively to pursue the sale of SingPost Centre. The postal and e-commerce logistics services provider posted an underlying net loss of S$500,000 in 2H FY 2025, notes the brokerage, which trims the target price to S$0.74 from S$0.77 while maintaining the buy rating. Shares are 2.7% higher at S$0.575. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0511 GMT - Singapore Airlines' passenger yields appears to be holding up, Citi Research analysts say in a note. This provided a boost to its full-year core earnings which slightly beat Citi's estimates. Incremental de-escalation in tariff tensions will likely help passenger pricing improve, and the relatively high payout ratio will likely support the share price, they add. The high payout could indicate delays in spending given on-going supply chain tightness, or higher confidence that Air India will not require further injections in the short term, they add. A key risk would be softer-than-expected passenger demand, ticket and cargo pricing, they add. Citi retains a neutral rating with a target price of S$6.34. Shares are 0.4% lower at S$6.85. (kimberley.kao@wsj.com)

data.title: Vesuvius' Four-month Performance In Line with Expectations
data.description:
Vesuvius said Friday its revenue and trading profit were in line with expectations for the four months ended April 30.

The molten metal flow engineering and technology company reported revenue broadly unchanged from the prior year, while trading profit declined year over year.

Looking ahead, the company expects full-year 2025 trading profit to come in slightly below its previous guidance and the prior year's level on a constant currency basis, though it cautioned that the outlook remains uncertain.

data.title: Market Chatter: Hillhouse Cashes In on BeiGene Rally; Backs Hengrui Pharma IPO
data.description:
Hillhouse Capital sold HK$2.08 billion worth of BeiGene shares at HK$130 each as it cashed in on the surge of the latter's shares, YiCai Global reported Thursday.

The sale trimmed Hillhouse Capital's stake in the drug company to 4.89% from 6.03%, according to the report.

Hillhouse Capital also joined GIC and UBS as a cornerstone investor in Jiangsu Hengrui Pharmaceuticals' (, HKG:1276) upcoming 13.1 billion-yuan secondary listing on the Hong Kong bourse, YiCai Global wrote.

data.title: Euro zone bond yields dip as trade deal excitement fizzles out
data.description:
Euro zone government bond yields dropped on Friday, backing further away from multi-week highs hit earlier this week as investors awaited further trade developments after risk appetite sparked by a de-escalation in the Sino-US trade war faded.

German 10-year bond yields (DE10YT=RR), the benchmark for the euro zone bloc, fell 3.3 basis points to 2.59%, but were still headed for their fourth consecutive weekly rise, reflecting the investor push away debt. Bond yields move inversely to prices.

The United States announced trade deals with the UK and China this month, allaying fears of a recession caused by the trade war, which lessened demand for safe-haven assets that sent U.S. and euro zone yields to one month highs this week.

However, a raft of soft data on Thursday indicated the world's largest economy slowed last month, prompted traders to up their bets on interest rate cuts from the Federal Reserve and sent U.S. 10-year Treasury yields (US10YT=TWEB) 7 bps lower.

The yield on the U.S. 10-year Treasury bond was down 4.7 bps at 4.40% in Europe on Friday.

Germany's two-year bond yield (DE2YT=RR), which is more sensitive to European Central Bank rate expectations, was down 1.8 bps at 1.865%.

Italy's 10-year yield fell 3.4 bps at 3.6%, and the gap between Italian and German bunds (DE10IT10=RR) narrowed 1.5 bps to 100.7 bps.

data.title: NetLink NBN Trust's DPU Rises 1.1% in Fiscal H2
data.description:
NetLink NBN Trust's distribution per unit or DPU rose 1.1% during the fiscal second half ended March 31 to SG$0.0268 from SG$0.0265 a year earlier, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Thursday.

Distributions attributable were up 1.1% to SG$104.4 million from SG$103.3 million a year earlier.

Revenue, however, declined 1.8% year over year to SG$202.2 million from SG$206.0 million, primarily due to lower ancillary project revenue.

data.title: REG-Major Shareholder Announcement
data.description:
Pursuant to Section 30 of the Danish Capital Markets Act, Svitzer Group A/S, CVR-no. 44 79 14 47, (“Svitzer”) hereby announces the receipt of the following major shareholder notifications following the announcement of the final result and completion of APMH Invest A/S’ all-cash voluntary recommended purchase offer to acquire all issued shares of Svitzer, excluding Svitzer treasury shares, if any, and shares held by APMH Invest A/S, on the terms set out in the offer document published by APMH Invest A/S on 2 April 2025.

On 16 May 2025, APMH Invest A/S has (on behalf of itself and its ultimate owner A.P. Møller og Hustru Chastine Mc-Kinney Møllers Fond til almene Formaal) notified Svitzer that APMH Invest A/S holds a total of 29,462,147 shares and voting rights in Svitzer corresponding to 93.4% of the total share capital and 93.4% of the voting rights in Svitzer. APMH Invest A/S is indirectly wholly owned by A.P. Møller og Hustru Chastine Mc-Kinney Møllers Fond til almene Formaal through A.P. Møller Holding A/S.

On 16 May 2025, A.P. Møller og Hustru Chastine McKinney Møllers Familiefond has notified Svitzer that A.P. Møller og Hustru Chastine McKinney Møllers Familiefond no longer holds any shares and voting rights in Svitzer.

About Svitzer

Svitzer is a leading, global towage and marine services provider. The core business is to assist large seaborne vessels in manoeuvring in and out of ports and terminals to berth and unberth. With around 450 vessels, Svitzer’s services play a crucial role as part of critical port infrastructure. Svitzer was founded in 1833 and serves approximately 2,000 customers in more than 140 ports and 40 terminals across 37 countries. Read more on www.svitzer.com.

For further information, please contact:

Michael Nass Nielsen, Head of Investor Relations and FP&A

T: +45 24941654

E: ir@svitzer.com

Attachment

  • Svitzer Group - Major Shareholder announcement

Notes:

1.

Rule 8 of the Takeover Code (the "Code") sets out the circumstances in which Dealing Disclosures and/or Opening Position Disclosures are required to be made by the parties to an offer, persons acting in concert with them, persons with an interest in relevant securities of 1% or more and exempt principal traders ("EPTs") connected with the parties to an offer.

2.

Where an offeror is marked "Disclosure of dealings and positions in this offeror is not required", dealings and positions in relevant securities of that offeror are not required to be disclosed under Rule 8. This is either because it has been announced that the offer or possible offer is, or is likely to be, solely in cash or because disclosure is not required for some other reason.

3.

Dealings and positions required to be disclosed under Rule 8 of the Code include dealings and positions in derivatives referenced to, and options in respect of, relevant securities of an offeree company and, where appropriate, an offeror (see Note 2 above).

4.

The information set out in the Disclosure Table relating to the relevant securities in issue of an offeree company or of an offeror (and any amendments thereto) has been extracted from the relevant announcement made by such company under Rule 2.9 of the Code. Similarly, details of any deadline set under Rule 2.6 by which a potential offeror must announce either a firm intention to make a bid or that it does not intend to make an offer (or, in the case of Rule 2.6(a), by which the Panel must consent to an extension of the deadline) has been extracted from the relevant announcement made by the offeree company.

5.

Additions and deletions of offeree companies and offerors are reflected in the Disclosure Table throughout the day, following the relevant announcement. Other amendments may also be made throughout the day. In any event, details of the classes of relevant securities issued by an offeree or offeror company, the number of such securities in issue ("NSI") and the International Securities Identification Number ("ISIN") of such securities announced under Rule 2.9 of the Code before 5.00 pm each day will be included in the final version of the Disclosure Table by 5.30 pm that day. The final version of the Disclosure Table issued each day will be marked "Final" and persons required to make Dealing Disclosures and Opening Position Disclosures are advised to use that final version. Any changes to the Rule 2.6 deadline of a potential offeror will also be reflected in the "Final" version of the Disclosure Table, although any such changes will not be shown in the "Amendments" section at the top of the Disclosure Table.

6.

Dealing Disclosures by the parties to an offer, persons acting in concert with them and connected EPTs must be made by no later than 12 noon on the business day (as defined in the Code) following the date of the relevant dealing and Dealing Disclosures by persons with an interest in relevant securities of 1% or more must be made by 3.30 pm on the business day following the date of the relevant dealing.

7.

Opening Position Disclosures by the parties to an offer and by connected EPTs must be made by no later than 12 noon on the 10th business day after the commencement of the offer period or the announcement that first identifies an offeror (as the case may be). Where required, Opening Position Disclosures by persons with interests in relevant securities of 1% or more must be made by no later than 3.30 pm on the 10th business day after the commencement of the offer period or the announcement that first identifies an offeror (as the case may be). Details of the time and date at which an offer period commenced and at which an offeror was first identified are set out in the Disclosure Table. In order to calculate the deadline for Opening Position Disclosures, it is therefore necessary to count forward 10 business days from the relevant date.

8.

Opening Position Disclosures and Dealing Disclosures required under Rule 8 of the Code must be made to a Regulatory Information Service ("RIS").

9.

This Note is applicable if it says "See Note 9 below" by the name of the company in the Disclosure Table above.  The Executive has granted the company a dispensation from the requirements under the Code that announcements must be published via a RIS. The company is instead required to publish all announcements on its website. The Executive has also granted a dispensation from Note 3 on Rule 8 of the Code that disclosures made under Rule 8 of the Code must be made to a RIS. Therefore, Opening Position Disclosures and Dealing Disclosures required under Rule 8 of the Code may be made to the company at the address stated in the announcement that commenced the offer period.

10.

The following are approved Regulatory Information Services:

data.title: LT Foods' Consolidated Profit Rises in Fiscal Q4, Shares Up 3%
data.description:
LT Foods posted an increase in its consolidated attributable profit to 1.61 billion rupees in the fiscal fourth quarter ended March 31, against 1.49 billion rupees a year ago.

Earnings per share came in at 4.62 rupees compared with the year-ago figure of 4.28 rupees, according to a Thursday filing to the Indian stock exchanges by the food products company.

Revenue from operations in fiscal Q4 also rose to 22.3 billion rupees from 20.7 billion rupees. a year earlier.

The company's board recommended a final equity dividend of 1 rupee per equity share of face value of 1 rupee each for the fiscal year ended March 31

LT Foods' shares were up nearly 3% in recent trade.

data.title: SATS to Invest Over SG$250 Million to Upgrade Operations, Infrastructure at Singapore's Changi Airport
data.description:
SATS outlined plans to invest around SG$250 million to upgrade its ground operations and cargo handling infrastructure at the Changi Airport, Singapore, a filing with the Singapore Exchange said on Thursday.

The investment includes an expenditure of over SG$150 million on the renewal and expansion of SG Hub's ground support equipment fleet.

Meanwhile, SG$100 million will be spent to improve cargo operations, the filing said.

data.title: IRFC accepts bids for 5-year bonds, bankers say
data.description:
Indian Railway Finance Corp (IRFC) has accepted bids worth 30 billion rupees ($350.6 million) for bonds maturing in 5 years, three bankers said on Friday.

It will pay a coupon of 6.65% and had invited commitment bids for the issue earlier in the day, they said.

The company did not immediately respond to a Reuters email seeking comment.

Here is the list of deals reported so far on May 16:

data.title: Swiss Re seen rising in premarket after Q1 beat
data.description:
** Shares in Swiss Re are seen edging up 1% in premarket indications after the reinsurer's Q1 net income came above analysts' expectations

** Swiss Re posts net income of $1.3 billion vs $938 million seen in a consensus forecast

** "One should not overemphasize the 36% consensus beat - but numbers are above the pro-rata full year target, which is clearly a positive," Vontobel's analyst Simon Foessmeier says in a note

** He adds the numbers compare favourably vs the two German peers that reported earlier this week, Hannover Re and Munich Re , and the shares should react positively

data.title: 2cureX CEO To Step Down
data.description:
2cureX AB :

  • SAID ON THURSDAY CO LAUNCHES NEW INITIATIVE FOR GLOBAL CLINICAL AND COMMERCIAL EXPANSION

  • CORNERSTONE INVESTOR PROVIDES SEK 4.5 MLN LOAN AS PART OF BROADER FINANCING COMMITMENT

  • IN LINE WITH THIS, DAN HONEYWELL WILL STEP DOWN AS CEO, EFFECTIVE 15 MAY 2025

  • NATHANIEL HUTLEY WILL ASSUME ROLE OF INTERIM CEO WHILE BOARD CONDUCTS DISCUSSIONS WITH CANDIDATES

data.title: PCG Powers Hong Kong's Mega-Event Economy and Participates in InnoEX 2025
data.description:
HONG KONG, May 16, 2025 - (ACN Newswire) - The Payment Cards Group Limited ("PCG)", a cloud-native payment processor and acquirer, and its members achieved multiple milestones in April this year, actively empowering Hong Kong's innovation and technology sector as well as its mega-event economy through innovative payment technologies. BBMSL, the payment solutions provider under PCG, was honored as "Best Digital Payment Service Provider" at the EDigest Brand Awards 2025. PCG's digital payment acceptance business, Yedpay, continued to support various local mega events, including the Tsing Yi Bamboo Theater 2025 and the Cathay/HSBC Hong Kong Sevens 2025. Meanwhile, PCG's startup business, A3A, Asia's first cloud-based processing and settlement platform, was invited to participate in InnoEX 2025 and the Hong Kong ICT Awards (FinTech) 2024 Post-Award Networking Event.

BBMSL pioneers digital payment innovation, garnering industry recognition

Since officially joining the PCG Group in December 2024, BBMSL has continuously driven innovative development within the local payment ecosystem. Guided by its "Payment Beyond Imagination" hilosophy and "Merchant-First" strategy, BBMSL was recently honored as "Best Digital Payment Service Provider" by EDigest at the EDigest Brand Awards 2025, affirming BBMSL' exceptional contributions in providing innovative, secure payment solutions to small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs).

In terms of industry events, PCG was one of the Gold Sponsors at the GS1 Hong Kong Annual Members Dinner held on April 8. BBMSL represented PCG at the event, showcasing the latest digital payment solutions and engaging with over 500 industry leaders to explore collaboration opportunities.

Additionally, on April 13, BBMSL was one of the supporting organizations for the IOT Data Hackathon 2025 Awards Ceremony at InnoEX 2025, witnessing young industry talent leverage IoT data to create innovative solutions for smart business and smart living.

Recently, BBMSL was featured in an article by Capital HK, highlighting how the strategic integration between PCG and BBMSL is reshaping the payment ecosystem. Through the integration with A3A, BBMSL has successfully increased payment efficiency by 25%, while leveraging PCG's status as a card scheme-recognized acquirer to optimize processes and enhance fund security. Looking ahead, BBMSL and PCG will continue to collaborate closely to advance digital payment development in Hong Kong and usher in a new phase of comprehensive service.

For the full article, please visit: https://www.capital-hk.com/column/blogger-AvyYu-20250424

Yedpay supports mega-event economy, promoting a cashless society

Yedpay has continued to promote a cashless society in Hong Kong and has recently successfully supported several local mega events, which showcased the diverse applications of digital payment technology. For sporting events, Yedpay provided digital payment services for the Cathay/HSBC Hong Kong Sevens 2025 from March 28 to 30, which allowed spectators to experience smooth cashless transactions. In terms of cultural activities, Yedpay provided a full range of digital payment services for the activity booths at the Tsing Yi Bamboo Theater 2025 from April 9 to 13, including POS terminals and QR code payment solutions, which enabled citizens and tourists to enjoy the convenience of modern payment technology while experiencing the traditional bamboo theater culture.

Additionally, Yedpay provided digital payment services at the Coldplay and Nicholas Tse concerts held in April, which enabled fans to enjoy instant transactions and contributing to Hong Kong's development as a smart city.

A3A was invited to participate in InnoEX 2025 and the Hong Kong ICT Awards (FinTech) 2024 Post-Award Networking Event

Thanks to its innovative financial cloud solutions, A3A, a member of PCG, was awarded the "Hong Kong ICT Awards 2024: FinTech Award" last year. In April this year, A3A was invited to join two major innovation and technology events, demonstrating its leadership in the payment technology industry.

At InnoEX 2025, held from April 13 to 16, A3A was invited by Hong Kong's Digital Policy Office (DPO) to showcase its award-winning payment processing innovations and future developments. During the exhibition, the A3A team engaged in insightful conversations with numerous industry experts to explore future trends in payment technology and establish valuable cooperative relationships.

In the same month, A3A was invited by the Institute of Financial Technologists of Asia (IFTA) to attend the Hong Kong ICT Awards (FinTech) 2025 Kick-off Briefing & 2024 Post-Award Networking Event. During the event, the A3A team shared its strategic directions, including how A3A is redefining digital payments and secure data ecosystems, along with its plan to accelerate growth and adoption of its cloud payment processing platform.

These key participations not only showcase PCG's technological capability, but also lay a solid foundation for future development. Moving forward, PCG will continue to collaborate with its enterprise members to enhance payment solutions, providing safer and more efficient payment services, and promoting Hong Kong's vision of becoming a cashless society.

About Payment Cards Group ("PCG")

The Payment Cards Group Limited ("PCG") is an innovative and leading payment technology company with operations in Singapore, Hong Kong and the Asia-Pacific region. Established in 2016, PCG has become an acquirer with principal memberships in all major card schemes and e-wallet networks. Yedpay, a member of PCG, has firmly established itself as a digital payment acceptance business in Hong Kong. Meanwhile, A3A, another member of PCG, has developed a cloud-native payment processing platform that operates through RESTful APIs, significantly reducing costs and streamlining complex processes while providing users with real-time transaction data and insights. As an acquiring processor, PCG serves as the backbone infrastructure of the entire payment industry by its Asia's 1st cloud-based processing and settlement platform. Rooted in Hong Kong with a global vison, PCG seeks to empower merchants with cutting-edge payment technology solutions and drive high-quality development in the global payment ecosystem. For more information, please visit PCG's website: https://www.yedpay.com/en/

For media enquiries, please contact:

The Payment Cards Group Limited

Alice Siu

Email: alice.siu@a3a.global

Tel: (852) 9192 8145

data.title: Swiss Re's Beat Is Low Quality — Market Talk
data.description: Swiss Re profits beat consensus comfortably but this was driven by a higher investment result and lighter-than-expected natural catastrophe losses, Keefe, Bruyette & Woods says after the reinsurer's first-quarter results. The combined ratio of its property and casualty reinsurance division was higher than consensus, which could be explained by the nominal catastrophe losses being lower than expectations despite being higher than what was budgeted. "Similarly to the other reinsurers, we think the market may be disappointed by the April renewals," analyst Darius Satkauskas writes in a research note. He also notes that though the headline price rose by 1.5%, the loss cost assumptions rose 3.7%, which leads to a negative 2.3% net price change. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

data.title: Alliance Global's Consolidated Net Income Soars 66% in Q1
data.description:
Alliance Global Group's consolidated net income for the first quarter surged 66% to 11 billion Philippine pesos, according to a Thursday Philippine bourse filing.

Consolidated revenues of the real estate and food and beverage company rose 3% annually to 51.9 billion pesos from 50.6 billion pesos, it said.

data.title: Harbin Pharmaceutical to List Idle Buildings for Lease; Shares Up 4%
data.description:
Harbin Pharmaceutical Group plans to publicly list certain idle buildings for lease, according to a Friday disclosure on the Shanghai bourse.

The buildings, with a land area of 64,868.20 square meters, are owned by the Chinese pharmaceutical company's branch, Harbin Pharmaceutical Group Sixth Pharmaceutical Factory, in Daowai District, Harbin, China.

The planned lease term is up to 10 years, the pharmaceutical company said.

The company's shares jumped 4% in recent trade.
